name: Kinder Morgan, Inc.

profile.description: Kinder Morgan, Inc. is the largest energy infrastructure company in North America, operating ~83,000 miles of pipelines and 139 terminals. Transports natural gas, crude oil, CO2, and refined products.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (0/3): Infrastructure operator. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI. Market Share (2/2): Largest N.A. energy infrastructure. Product Pipeline (0/2): Incremental expansion. R&D Intensity (1/1): Pipeline technology investment.

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Energy infrastructure operation. Community Impact (1/2): Community programs. Employee Retention (0/2): No standout data. Insider Buying (1/2): Rich Kinder maintains large personal stake.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored as Dividend. D/E 98.84% (0.88). Forward P/E 21.10 (0.97). ROE 10.60% (0.71). Revenue growth 13.8% (1.0). Profit margin 18.92% (0.95). Revenue 5yr CAGR 5% (0.67, 2x). Dividend: 10yr paid (2x), 7yr growth, 3% CAGR (2x), 70% payout (0.50).

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Physical pipeline infrastructure is immune to AI. AI data centers increase natural gas demand for power generation. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI-driven energy demand directly benefits KMI.

infinite_game.summary: KMI is a stable infrastructure operator with Rich Kinder as founder-chairman providing long-term orientation.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Uses Williams and Enterprise as benchmarks.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Adapted to natural gas from oil.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Maintained infrastructure investment through price cycles.

investment_thesis: Largest N.A. energy infrastructure at 21.10x forward P/E with 3.8% yield and 18.9% profit margin. AI data center demand drives natural gas consumption. Pipeline assets have replacement value far exceeding market cap.
thesis_continued: Forward P/E of 21.10 is elevated for a midstream company. Revenue 5yr CAGR of just 5% reflects mature growth. D/E of 98.8% and 70% payout ratio limit flexibility. Energy transition could reduce long-term pipeline demand.
key_catalysts: AI data center power demand driving natural gas consumption growth
Dividend growth from improving free cash flow
key_risks: Forward P/E of 21x is premium for a midstream company with 5% 5yr growth CAGR
D/E of 99% and 70% payout ratio constrain financial flexibility
Long-term energy transition could reduce pipeline demand over decades
sources: Kinder Morgan Q1 2026 Earnings — SEC Filings, Yahoo Finance, 2026
